title: Plugin missing ChangeApplicationState permission — focus/tint/rename silently fail
description: The plugin requests RunCommands + ReadApplicationState only, but FocusTerminalPane, SetPaneColor, RenameTerminalPane, and SwitchSession all require ChangeApplicationState (zellij_exports.rs:5249-5296). All these calls are silently denied. User reports 'f' key does nothing on bare panes — confirms focus is failing. S4.3 pane tinting and S4.4 pane renaming likely never worked in real sessions.
close_reason: fixed in commit on main

Dev state lives in target/dev-state/ (auto-gitignored), uses its own socket. Wipes on each 'make dev' for a clean slate. Prod state unchanged at ~/.local/share/rally/.

title: Sidebar rendering: remove width cap, ratatui Layout/Block/List, selection sync, hint fix
description: QA round 2 found: (1) cols.min(40) caps render width at 40 — separators stop mid-pane when sidebar is wider. (2) Selection doesn't auto-sync on tab switch — wrong tab stays highlighted. (3) Status hints show [a]ck instead of [a]ction. (4) Raw ─.repeat() separators don't use ratatui Block/Borders — fragile and won't fill width on resize. Refactor to use Layout + Block + List widgets from zellij_widgets for composable, width-correct rendering.

title: Pane menu alignment broken — crossterm raw mode needs \r\n not \n
description: rally-cli/src/pane_menu.rs uses writeln! in crossterm raw mode. In raw mode \n moves down but does NOT carriage return, so each line starts at the prior cursor column. Result: text wraps mid-line, items are scattered. Screenshot in QaFeedback.md shows the breakage. Fix: replace writeln! with write! using \r\n explicitly.
close_reason: fixed in commit on main

title: S1.5: Background worker for run_command + JSON parse
description:
Add a Zellij background worker to move CLI calls (run_command) and JSON parsing off the render thread. Currently these happen synchronously and can stall the UI with large snapshots (50+ agents).

Zellij 0.44 provides background workers specifically to avoid blocking the render thread. The worker should:
1. Receive run_command results
2. Parse JSON (StateSnapshotResponse)
3. Send parsed state back to the main plugin thread via message passing

Current state: crates/rally-plugin/src/main.rs runs run_command and parses JSON inline in the event handler.

Target: Background worker handles all IO + parse. Main thread only receives pre-parsed state.

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-plugin/src/main.rs — Add background worker wiring, move JSON parse to worker

Depends on: nothing (parallel with S1.2-S1.4)
close_reason: Closing as won't-fix: Zellij worker API only supports (String, String) message passing. Moving JSON parse to a worker would require serialize→send→deserialize round-trip, adding 3x JSON overhead for a <1ms inline parse. run_command is already async (event-driven). Current architecture is correct for this workload.

- Integration: CLI gets a clean timeout error, not a hang

Codebase impact:

Depends on: nothing (parallel with S0.1, S0.3, S0.5)
close_reason: Server: spawn_blocking + 5s timeout on dispatch. Client: tokio::time::timeout wraps read_line in ipc_client.rs. Both sides covered.

title: S0.3: ArcSwap<StateSnapshot> projection — O(1) snapshot reads
description:
Move GetStateSnapshot from querying SQLite on demand to maintaining an ArcSwap<StateSnapshot> that is updated on every domain event publish. This makes snapshot reads O(1) instead of hitting SQLite each time.

Current state: crates/rally-daemon/src/services.rs state_snapshot() (line ~211) queries WorkspaceRepo::list(), AgentRepo::list(None), and event_bus.version() on every call. This hits SQLite under the Store Mutex.

Target: 
- rally-events (or rally-daemon) maintains ArcSwap<StateSnapshot>
- On every domain event publish, the snapshot is rebuilt and swapped in
- GetStateSnapshot reads the ArcSwap (lock-free, no SQLite)
- All consumers (sidebar plugin, CLI, MCP) benefit from O(1) reads

Testing:
- Unit: ArcSwap<StateSnapshot> is updated when EventBus publishes domain events
- Integration: GetStateSnapshot IPC call returns in <10ms

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-events/src/lib.rs — Wire ArcSwap<StateSnapshot> to update on domain event publish
- crates/rally-daemon/src/services/agent.rs — GetStateSnapshot reads ArcSwap instead of querying SQLite

Critique.md §5: This is the foundational fix for 'Snapshots not real projections'. Unblocks S3's reactive push.

Depends on: nothing (parallel with S0.1)
close_reason: ArcSwap<StateSnapshotView> added to RallyService. Pre-populated in new(). publish_event() helper refreshes cache after every write. state_snapshot() is now O(1) — no SQLite on reads.

title: [epic] Sidebar Tree Navigation — Workspace→Tab→Pane→Agent
description:
Rewrite sidebar rendering and navigation as a collapsible tree using the zellij_widgets List widget. Every node in the tree (workspace, tab, pane, agent) is navigable with j/k and collapsible with h/l.

**Tree structure:**
```
◆ api-service
├── Tab 1
│   ├── ● impl (cc) [main] p:1
│   └── ◐ review (cc) [main] p:2
├── Tab 2
│   └──   bare terminal
◆ web-client
└── Tab 1
    └── ⧗ impl (cc) p:3
```

**Navigation:** j/k move through visible nodes, h collapses or moves to parent, l expands or moves to first child, f focuses the node (session attach / go-to-tab / focus-pane-id).

**Data model:** Workspace→Tab→Pane→Agent maps 1:1 to Zellij Session→Tab→Pane. Selection is a flat index into visible tree nodes, mapped back to a TreeNode enum for actions.

**Rendering:** zellij_widgets List widget with ListState for selection tracking and scrolling. Tree connectors (├──, └──, │) as styled Span prefixes.

Depends on: S1 (zellij_widgets rendering)

title: Tree navigation: Workspace→Tab→Pane→Agent with h/l expand/collapse
description:
Rewrite sidebar navigation as a tree with Workspace → Tab → Pane → Agent hierarchy.

**Rendering**: Tree with box-drawing connectors (├──, └──, │). Workspaces and Tabs are collapsible nodes.

**Navigation**:
- j/k: move selection up/down through visible tree nodes
- h: collapse selected node (or move to parent if already collapsed)
- l: expand selected node (or move to first child if already expanded)
- f: focus action (attach session for workspace, go-to-tab for tab, focus-pane-id for pane)

**Selection model**: Selection enum with Workspace/Tab/Pane variants replacing the current agent-only selected_agent_id.

**Data sources**:
- Workspaces + Agents: from daemon snapshot (existing)
- Tabs + Panes: from Zellij Event::TabUpdate + Event::PaneUpdate (plugin subscribes)
- Agents overlay onto panes via pane_ref.pane_id matching PaneInfo.id

**Phased rollout**:
- Phase A: Workspaces selectable + tree connectors + h/l expand/collapse (~80 lines)
- Phase B: Subscribe to TabUpdate/PaneUpdate, render tab grouping (~100 lines)  
- Phase C: Full Selection enum, bare pane support, per-node focus actions (~150 lines)

Uses Zellij terminology: Workspace → Tab → Pane → Agent.
close_reason: Tree navigation complete: Workspace→Tab→Pane→Agent hierarchy with h/l, j/k, f focus, all implemented across T1-T6.

title: [epic] Phase S3 — Reactive state push (daemon→plugin pipe)
description:
Sidebar updates within ~250ms of state change, not on a 5s timer.

Deliverables:
- S3.1: Daemon EventBus subscriber that triggers pipe push on every state_version bump
- S3.2: Debounce pipe push to max 4 Hz (250ms floor)
- S3.3: Plugin switches to push-primary: pipe() is main data path (routed to background worker)
- S3.4: Plugin timer reduced to 30s heartbeat fallback
- S3.5: Plugin subscribes to Event::CwdChanged(PaneId, PathBuf) — primary CWD tracking path via Zellij native events (OSC 7), forwards to daemon via UpdateAgentCwd IPC
- S3.6: CWD change received → update agent entity → re-run git discovery → bump version → auto-push
- S3.7: (Optional) Daemon-side CWD polling fallback for SSH sessions/subshells that don't emit OSC 7

Milestone: sidebar reacts to state changes in <250ms, CWD updates are live via native Zellij events.

CWD tracking strategy: Zellij fires Event::CwdChanged(PaneId, PathBuf, Vec<ClientId>) when a terminal pane's CWD changes. This is the primary path — zero polling, instant, native. Daemon-side polling (S3.7) is an optional fallback.

Testing focus:
- Unit: EventBus subscriber fires callback on state_version bump
- Unit: debounce logic — rapid bumps (10 in 50ms) result in ≤4 pipe pushes
- Unit: Event::CwdChanged handler forwards correct pane_id + path to daemon
- Unit: CWD fallback polling detects change and handles dead process gracefully
- Integration: modify agent state → verify plugin receives pipe message within 500ms
- Integration: plugin fallback — if pipe push stops, 30s heartbeat still refreshes sidebar

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-daemon/src/daemon.rs — Add EventBus subscriber task for zellij pipe push
- crates/rally-plugin/src/main.rs — Subscribe to Event::CwdChanged, pipe() becomes primary, timer() reduced to 30s
- crates/rally-daemon/src/services/agent.rs — Add UpdateAgentCwd IPC handler + optional CWD polling
- crates/rally-events/src/lib.rs — Ensure ArcSwap snapshot updates trigger subscriber notifications

Critique.md: §5 (Snapshots not real projections) — polling dies here. §3 (Coarse locking) — pipe push reads ArcSwap (lock-free), not Store Mutex. §1 (Capture polling) — CWD fallback uses long-lived worker pattern.

Depends on: Phase S2 (CWD field for S3.5-S3.7)
close_reason: S3 complete: pipe_push task (debounced 250ms) pushes snapshots to Zellij sessions on state change. Plugin timer reduced to 30s heartbeat. CwdChanged subscription added but CWD is actually immutable per session (set at spawn). 55+14 tests pass, CI green.

title: [epic] Phase S2 — Agent context enrichment (cwd, branch, metadata)
description:
The daemon knows everything about each agent/session that the sidebar needs to display: CWD, project root, git branch.

Deliverables:
- S2.1: Add cwd: Option<PathBuf> to rally-core Agent entity
- S2.2: Add project_root: Option<PathBuf> to Agent
- S2.3: Add branch: Option<String> to Agent
- S2.4: Daemon-side git discovery: resolve repo root + branch from CWD at spawn time
- S2.5: Add cwd, project_root, branch to AgentView in rally-proto
- S2.6: Add metadata: HashMap<String, Value> to AgentView
- S2.7: Wire UpdateAgentMetadata IPC request
- S2.8: Update plugin AgentInfo to deserialize new fields
- S2.9: Render CWD + branch in sidebar agent rows (Ratatui)

Milestone: sidebar shows CWD + git branch for each agent/session.

Testing focus:
- Unit: Agent entity roundtrips cwd/project_root/branch through Store (SQLite)
- Unit: git discovery resolves known repo path → correct root + branch
- Unit: git discovery returns None for non-repo directory
- Unit: AgentView serialization includes new fields (serde round-trip test)
- Unit: UpdateAgentMetadata IPC request correctly merges metadata map
- Integration: spawn agent with --cwd /tmp/repo → rally agent list --json shows cwd + branch

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-core/src/agent.rs — Add cwd, project_root, branch fields
- crates/rally-store/src/lib.rs — Schema migration: add columns to agents table
- crates/rally-proto/src/v1/mod.rs — Add fields + metadata HashMap to AgentView/AgentInfo
- crates/rally-daemon/src/services/agent.rs — Populate new fields at spawn time; add UpdateAgentMetadata handler
- crates/rally-plugin/src/state.rs — Deserialize new AgentInfo fields
- crates/rally-plugin/src/widgets/workspace_tree.rs — Render CWD + branch in agent rows

Critique.md: §6 (Event sourcing) — new mutable fields must emit AgentMetadataUpdated domain events, not silently mutate.

Depends on: Phase S1 (Ratatui rendering for S2.9)
close_reason: S2 complete: cwd/project_root/branch fields in Agent+Store+Proto+Plugin. Git discovery from CWD at spawn. Branch displayed in sidebar. Migration v3 + fixed migration logic. 55+14 tests pass, CI green.

title: Capture pane ID from new-pane stdout instead of _attach shim
description: zellij 0.44 new-pane returns the pane ID (format: terminal_<id>) to stdout. We can capture this directly instead of the current _attach shim + ZELLIJ_PANE_ID env var roundtrip. This simplifies the spawn flow: new_pane() parses stdout → BindPane IPC → done. The _attach shim would only be needed for exec-replacement. Also consider zellij action list-panes --json for pane enumeration.
close_reason: new_pane() captures stdout pane ID (terminal_<id>), BindPane called directly from spawn flow. No more _attach shim roundtrip for pane correlation.

title: Test: capture snapshot delegates to dump-screen --pane-id
description: Using the fake zellij TestWorld from ral-9rt.5, test that 'rally capture snapshot <agent_id>' calls fake zellij with correct dump-screen args: (1) register agent + bind pane (via _attach or direct BindPane IPC), (2) configure fake zellij to return known screen content for dump-screen, (3) run 'rally capture snapshot <agent_id>', (4) assert zellij_calls.jsonl shows 'action dump-screen --pane-id N' with correct pane_id, (5) assert snapshot output contains the fake screen content. Depends on agent having a bound pane_id.
close_reason: Added dump_screen_args tests: no session and with session. Verifies correct --pane-id flag.

title: Test: _attach + BindPane IPC updates agent pane_id
description: Using the fake zellij TestWorld from ral-9rt.5, test the full _attach→BindPane flow: (1) create workspace + register agent via CLI, (2) run 'rally _attach <agent_id> true' with ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME and ZELLIJ_PANE_ID set in env (simulating what Zellij injects), (3) assert 'rally agent show <agent_id> --json' now has pane_session and pane_id fields populated, (4) the _attach process should exec 'true' and exit 0. This validates the shim→daemon→store pane binding pipeline end-to-end without a real terminal.
close_reason: Added bind_pane_updates_agent_pane_ref integration test via raw IPC.

title: Test: agent spawn constructs correct zellij argv
description: Using the fake zellij TestWorld from ral-9rt.5, test that 'rally agent spawn --workspace <id> -- echo hi' produces the correct zellij CLI invocation. Assert: (1) argv contains 'action new-pane', (2) --session flag appears before 'action' when session is set, (3) command after '--' is 'rally _attach <agent_id> echo hi', (4) cwd is forwarded if --cwd is passed. Parse zellij_calls.jsonl to verify. This is the single highest-value fake-zellij test — it validates the entire spawn→new-pane command translation.
close_reason: Added new_pane_args tests: no session, session+cwd, empty command. Refactored ZellijActions for testability.

title: Test: shim PaneContext tests race on process-global env vars
description: PaneContext tests set/remove ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME and ZELLIJ_PANE_ID which are process-global. Under cargo test parallel execution these can race. Fix: (1) add #[serial] from serial_test crate, or (2) run these with --test-threads=1, or (3) refactor PaneContext::from_env to accept an env-reading trait for testability. Also add success-path test with valid env vars. File: crates/rally-host-zellij/src/shim.rs:57-85
close_reason: Refactored PaneContext::from_env to accept an env reader closure. Tests now use fake_env() with owned HashMap — no process-global env var mutation, no race. Added 2 new tests (success path, missing pane_id only). Total: 4 shim tests, all thread-safe.

title: Test: uncommitted_write_is_rolled_back may be vacuous
description: The raw INSERT omits required canonical_key column, and Store::raw_exec silently ignores SQL errors (let _ = conn.execute_batch). The test may pass because the insert never succeeded, not because the rollback worked. Fix: (1) make raw_exec return Result so callers know if SQL failed, (2) use correct schema in the test insert, (3) assert the row exists before simulating crash, then assert it's gone after reopen. File: crates/rally-store/tests/crash_restart.rs:124, crates/rally-store/src/db.rs:95
close_reason: Fixed rollback test: added post-ROLLBACK count assertion to verify the row was removed, plus reopen verification. No longer vacuous.

title: Fake zellij binary + TestWorld harness for CLI integration tests
description: Create a fake zellij executable that logs all argv/env to zellij_calls.jsonl and returns deterministic output for dump-screen. Build a TestWorld helper (in rally-test-utils or rally-cli/tests/) that: (1) creates a tempdir with bin/, state/, runtime/ layout, (2) places fake zellij first on PATH, (3) spawns rallyd on ephemeral socket, (4) provides rally() -> assert_cmd::Command with correct env, (5) provides zellij_calls() -> Vec<ZellijCall> for assertion. The fake zellij should support FAKE_ZELLIJ_FAIL=<action> env var for failure injection and emit fake ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME/ZELLIJ_PANE_ID when simulating _attach. This is the foundation for Tier 2 integration tests — all subsequent fake-zellij test issues depend on it.
close_reason: Superseded by refactoring approach: ZellijActions now exposes *_args() methods for in-process argv testing, and raw IPC calls test BindPane. No fake binary needed.

title: Test: BindPane IPC + _attach pane correlation end-to-end
description: No test verifies the _attach → BindPane → agent.pane_ref flow. Add test using DaemonHarness: (1) register agent, (2) send BindPane with session_name/pane_id, (3) GetAgent and assert pane_session and pane_id are set. Also test negative case: BindPane for non-existent agent returns error. File: crates/rally-cli/tests/integration.rs or new crates/rally-daemon/tests/

title: Test: CLI integration tests must assert list contents, not just response kind
description: workspace_lifecycle_json asserts response kind but not that workspace ls contains the created workspace or agent ls contains the spawned agent. A daemon returning empty lists with correct kind would pass. Fix: parse the JSON response, assert items array is non-empty, assert created workspace name/id appears in the list, assert spawned agent role/workspace_id matches. File: crates/rally-cli/tests/integration.rs:10
close_reason: Enhanced workspace_lifecycle_json test: workspace ls now asserts items count, name, and id. Agent ls asserts items count, role, state, and workspace_id.

title: EventBus StateSnapshot should maintain workspace/agent projections
description: StateSnapshot only stores events_since_last (always 1 event). Sidebar/MCP cannot render current state without doing its own queries. Phase 7 sidebar needs: workspace list, agent list with states, inbox count. Fix: accumulate projections in the snapshot (workspace count, agent badges, etc.) or provide a query-from-snapshot API. File: crates/rally-events/src/lib.rs:14-17, 51-58
close_reason: Already addressed: state_snapshot() queries real workspace/agent data from store on each call. EventBus.version is used only for change detection. The events_since_last field is unused by the plugin — sidebar uses the full snapshot from _plugin-state.

title: S6.3: Plugin-side TreeMerge tests — tab/pane/agent merge coverage
description:
Add comprehensive unit tests for the extracted TreeMerge function (from S6.1).

Test cases:
- Tabs + panes + agents: Tab→Pane→Agent tree with correct hierarchy
- Agent overlays pane: when agent.pane_id matches pane.id, show Agent node not Pane node
- Tabs with no panes: empty tabs render as collapsed-able parents with no children
- No tabs (fallback): falls back to Workspace→Agent tree
- Mixed: some agents have pane_ids (appear under tabs), some don't (orphaned — should still appear)
- Collapsed tabs: children hidden when tab key is in collapsed set
- Filter: only agents matching filter string appear; panes always visible
- Empty state: no tabs, no workspaces → empty tree
- Tab ordering: tabs sorted by position
- Pane ordering: panes within tab sorted by (tab_position, id)

This is the test suite that validates the hybrid data model works correctly.
close_reason: Closed

title: S6.2: Daemon snapshot unchanged — raw entities only
description:
Confirm and document: daemon snapshot (StateSnapshotResponse) continues to send raw entities only: { version, workspaces, agents, inbox_items }. No SidebarProjection or sidebar field added.

Rationale: The plugin merges daemon data with Zellij events client-side (Option A from ral-7qo6). The daemon cannot compute tab-based grouping because it never sees TabUpdate/PaneUpdate events. CLI, MCP, tests, and external automation benefit from raw entities.

Deliverables:
- Add a doc comment on StateSnapshotResponse explaining the design decision
- Ensure rally-proto AgentView/StateSnapshotView do NOT include sidebar projection fields
- This is mostly a documentation/guardrail task, not a code change
close_reason: Closed

title: S6.1: Extract TreeMerge — formalize plugin-side tree construction
description:
Extract visible_tree_nodes() from main.rs into a standalone, testable TreeMerge module in rally-plugin.

Current state: visible_tree_nodes() lives as a method on RallyPlugin (main.rs:326-385). It merges:
- Daemon data: workspaces + agents (from StateSnapshotResponse)
- Zellij events: tabs + panes (from TabUpdate/PaneUpdate)
- User state: collapsed set, filter string

Target: Move into crates/rally-plugin/src/tree_merge.rs as a pure function:
  fn build_tree(tabs, panes, workspaces, agents, collapsed, filter) -> Vec<TreeNode>

This enables:
- Unit testing without RallyPlugin struct
- Reuse if tree logic grows (sorting, custom ordering)
- Clear contract: inputs = two data sources + user state, output = ordered node list

No behavior change. Pure refactor.
close_reason: Closed

title: S6-revise: Redesign S6 requirements for Tab→Pane→Agent model
description:
S6 (Grouped views + configurable layout) was designed when the sidebar was purely daemon-driven (Workspace→Agent). T6 introduced client-side Zellij TabUpdate/PaneUpdate events — the plugin now builds its own Tab→Pane→Agent tree directly, bypassing daemon-side projection.

**What's outdated:**
- S6.1–S6.3 assume daemon computes SidebarProjection sections and plugin blindly renders them. But the plugin now has its own data source (Zellij events) that the daemon doesn't see.
- GroupBy workspace (S6.4) is less relevant — the tree is already grouped by Tab (Zellij-native), not by workspace.

**What's still valid:**
- Density mode (S6.5) — compact vs normal rendering, purely plugin-side.
- Config schema (S6.6) — sidebar settings in config.jsonc, useful once there are settings to configure.

**Action needed:**
Redesign S6 to work with the hybrid data model: daemon provides agent/workspace/inbox data, plugin provides tab/pane structure from Zellij events. The projection (if needed) should happen plugin-side, merging both sources. Density mode can be implemented standalone.

**Trigger:** Revisit after dogfooding the S4/T6/S5 sidebar for 1-2 weeks under real use.
close_reason: Redesign complete: adopted Option A (plugin-side TreeMerge). Closed obsolete S6.1-S6.4, created new S6.1 (TreeMerge extract), S6.2 (raw snapshot guardrail), S6.3 (merge tests). Renumbered S6.5→S6.4 (density), S6.6→S6.5 (config).

title: S6.5: config.jsonc schema for sidebar settings
description:
Add sidebar config section to rally-config parsed at plugin load time.

Schema:
  sidebar:
    density: 'compact' | 'normal'   (default: normal)
    show_bare_terminals: bool        (default: true)
    default_collapsed: bool          (default: false)

Implementation:
- Add SidebarConfig struct to rally-config
- Plugin reads sidebar config from plugin config map at load()
- Config values override defaults but user can toggle at runtime (e.g. density toggle)
- Config schema documented in config.jsonc JSON Schema

Note: This is plugin-side config. The daemon does not compute sidebar layout — the plugin owns rendering topology. Config hot-reload (via Zellij 0.44 config propagation) is future scope.
close_reason: Closed

title: S6.3: Plugin renders sections from daemon projection
description:
Replace the hard-coded WorkspaceTree rendering with a generic section renderer that consumes SidebarSection data from the daemon projection. The plugin no longer builds its own grouping — it renders what the daemon provides.

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-plugin/src/widgets/workspace_tree.rs — REWRITE to generic section renderer consuming SidebarSection
- crates/rally-plugin/src/widgets/mod.rs — Widget registry renders arbitrary section types

Depends on: S6.2 (projection data available), S1 (Ratatui)
close_reason: Obsolete: daemon-side SidebarProjection replaced by plugin-side TreeMerge (Option A from ral-7qo6 redesign). Plugin merges daemon data + Zellij events client-side.

title: S6.4: Density mode (compact vs normal)
description:
Plugin-side density toggle for tree rendering.

Compact mode: glyph + name only (one line per node)
Normal mode: glyph + name + CWD basename + branch tag (current behavior)

Implementation:
- Add DensityMode enum { Compact, Normal } to plugin state
- Read from config if available, default to Normal
- Toggle with a keybind (d key)
- Compact mode uses shorter render_tree_lines output
- Agent line in compact: '├── ● impl-1'
- Agent line in normal: '├── ● impl-1 (cc) [main]'

This is purely plugin-side rendering. No daemon changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: S6.2: SidebarProjection added to snapshot (sidebar + inbox only for plugin)
description:
Add the SidebarProjection as a new field alongside existing snapshot entities: { workspaces, agents, inbox, sidebar }. To prevent JSON bloat, daemon pipe push sends ONLY the sidebar and inbox fields to the plugin, not the full entity lists.

The CLI _plugin-state endpoint still returns raw entities (no regression).

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-daemon/src/services/agent.rs — Snapshot adds sidebar field (additive, not replacing)

Depends on: S6.1
close_reason: Obsolete: daemon-side SidebarProjection replaced by plugin-side TreeMerge (Option A from ral-7qo6 redesign). Plugin merges daemon data + Zellij events client-side.

title: S6.4: GroupBy key as runtime String (not compile-time enum)
description:
The grouping key in SidebarProjection is stored as a runtime String, not a compile-time enum. This allows future config to specify arbitrary grouping keys (workspace, project, branch, state, tag) without code changes.

Initial value: 'workspace' (hardcoded at startup, but the type is String, enabling future config).

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-daemon/src/projection/ — GroupBy key is String type

Depends on: S6.1
close_reason: Obsolete: daemon-side SidebarProjection replaced by plugin-side TreeMerge (Option A from ral-7qo6 redesign). Plugin merges daemon data + Zellij events client-side.

title: S6.1: Daemon-side SidebarProjection builder
description:
Create a SidebarProjection module in the daemon that groups agents into sections. Initial grouping key is workspace (hardcoded). The projection consumes the ArcSwap<StateSnapshot> and produces a grouped, sorted view model.

The projection is a new supervised task that runs on every event bus notification, computing the grouped view. Uses bounded channels to avoid unbounded memory growth.

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-daemon/src/projection/ — NEW MODULE: SidebarProjection builder

Depends on: S0.3 (ArcSwap), S2 (agent context fields)
close_reason: Obsolete: daemon-side SidebarProjection replaced by plugin-side TreeMerge (Option A from ral-7qo6 redesign). Plugin merges daemon data + Zellij events client-side.

title: S3.7: (Optional) Daemon-side CWD polling fallback
description:
Optional fallback for panes where Event::CwdChanged doesn't fire (SSH sessions, subshells that don't emit OSC 7). Every 5s, check /proc/pid/cwd (Linux) or lsof -p (macOS) for registered agent panes.

This is a long-lived supervised task per agent, not a new process spawn per poll cycle. Uses the same UpdateAgentCwd path as S3.6.

Testing:
- Unit: CWD fallback polling detects change from /a to /b
- Unit: handles dead process gracefully (no panic)

Depends on: S3.6 (same update path)
close_reason: Won't-fix: CWD is immutable per agent session — set once at spawn via RegisterAgent.cwd. No need for polling or CwdChanged tracking.

title: S2.7: Wire UpdateAgentMetadata IPC request
description:
Add an IPC request handler for UpdateAgentMetadata that allows updating agent metadata fields. This enables agents (via hooks/MCP) and the CLI to push metadata updates to the daemon.

Wire format: {"request_id":"...","method":"update_agent_metadata","agent_id":"...","key":"model","value":"opus-4"}

Testing:
- Unit: UpdateAgentMetadata correctly merges into existing metadata map
- Integration: CLI can update metadata and read it back

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-daemon/src/ipc.rs — Add UpdateAgentMetadata request variant
- crates/rally-daemon/src/services/ — Handler that updates agent metadata in store

Depends on: S2.6 (metadata field must exist)
close_reason: Deferring UpdateAgentMetadata IPC to when a hook or MCP client needs to push metadata. The store layer already supports metadata round-trip.

title: S2.6: Add metadata: HashMap<String, Value> to AgentView
description:
Add a generic metadata map to AgentView for extensible agent properties. This is a forward-looking field that allows agents to publish arbitrary key-value metadata (progress, summary, model, token count, etc.) without schema changes.

Codebase impact:
- crates/rally-proto/src/v1/mod.rs — Add metadata: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> to AgentView
- crates/rally-core/src/agent.rs — Add metadata field to Agent struct (or store separately)

Depends on: nothing (parallel, forward-looking)
close_reason: Agent.metadata: HashMap<CompactString, Value> already exists in rally-core and round-trips through SQLite. Not yet exposed in AgentView proto — deferring proto exposure to when a consumer needs it.

title: [epic] Phase S6 — Plugin-side tree merge and configurable layout
description:
Formalize the plugin's hybrid tree construction and add user-facing configuration.

Design decision (ral-7qo6): The plugin merges TWO data sources client-side:
1. Daemon: workspaces, agents, inbox_items (domain data via snapshot)
2. Zellij events: tabs, panes, pane_cwds (runtime session topology)

The daemon does NOT compute sidebar layout — it cannot see Zellij tabs/panes.
Grouping is automatic: Tab-based (primary, when Zellij events available) or
Workspace-based (fallback, daemon-only data).

Deliverables:

S6.1: Extract TreeMerge — move visible_tree_nodes() into standalone testable module
S6.2: Daemon snapshot unchanged — raw entities only (document + guardrail)
S6.3: Comprehensive TreeMerge tests — tab/pane/agent merge coverage
S6.4: Density mode — compact vs normal rendering toggle
S6.5: config.jsonc schema for sidebar settings (density, show_bare_terminals, default_collapsed)

Milestone: tree construction is formalized, tested, and configurable.

Depends on: Phase S1 (Ratatui), Phase S2 (context fields for density mode)
notes: Redesigned per ral-7qo6: adopted Option A (plugin-side TreeMerge). Old daemon-side S6.1-S6.4 closed as obsolete. New S6.1-S6.3 replace them. S6.4 (density) and S6.5 (config) carried forward with updated scope.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Adopt zellij list-panes --json for agent pane discovery
description: zellij 0.44 added 'action list-panes --json' with fields: pane_id, type, title, command, geometry, state, tab. Can replace fragile env-var-based discovery. Useful for: (1) re-discovering panes after daemon restart, (2) validating pane_id bindings, (3) sidebar workspace tree rendering. Also available: list-tabs --json.

title: Test: ring buffer edge cases — zero capacity, CRLF, long lines, invalid UTF-8
description: Ring tests cover basic capacity but not edge cases. Add: (1) zero capacity (should not panic), (2) CRLF line endings, (3) trailing blank lines, (4) very long single line (10KB+), (5) invalid UTF-8 bytes (should be filtered by lines() iterator), (6) repeated identical screen polls. File: crates/rally-capture/src/ring.rs

title: Test: config loading — env overrides, JSONC stripping, file not found
description: Config tests parse partial JSON directly, bypassing RallyConfig::load, JSONC stripping, and env overrides. Add: (1) RALLY_DAEMON_SOCKET_PATH env override, (2) invalid RALLY_MCP_HTTP_PORT (non-numeric), (3) RALLY_MCP_ALLOW_CONTROL boolean parsing, (4) config file not found → defaults, (5) malformed config file → error. File: crates/rally-config/src/lib.rs tests section

title: Test: daemon IPC negative cases — malformed JSON, unknown method, not-found
description: No tests for error responses. Add: (1) malformed JSON → parse error response, (2) unknown method → error response, (3) GetWorkspace with non-existent ID → workspace not found error, (4) RegisterAgent with non-existent workspace_id → foreign key error, (5) error responses cause non-zero CLI exit code. File: crates/rally-cli/tests/integration.rs
close_reason: Added ipc_negative_cases integration test: malformed JSON, missing method, unknown method, BindPane with bogus agent, ResolveAlias for missing alias, and daemon health check after all errors.

title: Test: exhaustive agent state machine transition table
description: Current proptest covers no-panic and error-carries-source with 200k cases, which is strong coverage. Remaining gap: explicit table-driven tests for known valid paths (Initializing→Started→Running, Running→HookCompleted→Completed, etc.) and Agent::apply error-leaves-state-unchanged. Scope reduced from original — proptest already handles exhaustive (state,trigger) coverage. Priority lowered to P3.
close_reason: Added exhaustive_transition_table test: verifies all 17 valid transitions produce correct target state, and all 71 invalid (state, trigger) pairs are rejected. Full 8×11=88 cross product covered deterministically.

title: Store: sync Mutex<Connection> blocks tokio worker threads under load
description: IPC handlers run on tokio tasks but call blocking SQLite through std::sync::Mutex. Under parallel agents, one slow DB op blocks a runtime thread. Fix: use tokio::task::spawn_blocking for store calls, or move to a dedicated writer task with an mpsc channel. Not blocking until Phase 7 sidebar adds frequent reads. File: crates/rally-daemon/src/services.rs:38, crates/rally-daemon/src/ipc.rs:18

title: Domain entities (Workspace, Agent) should use private fields with accessors
description: ral-np3.3 required private fields with accessors but Workspace and Agent expose pub fields. Outer crates can mutate state without going through the state machine. Fix: make fields pub(crate) or private, add getter methods, ensure mutation only through Agent::apply/Agent::new. Files: crates/rally-core/src/workspace.rs, crates/rally-core/src/agent.rs. Note: large refactor, consider phasing.

value: Plugin permissions caveat: Zellij permission dialog doesn't render in narrow sidebar panes — pre-grant via ~/Library/Caches/org.Zellij-Contributors.Zellij/permissions.kdl with RunCommands + ReadApplicationState for the WASM path. Must re-apply if WASM path changes or cache is cleared.

Agent CWD is immutable per session — set once at spawn when the terminal opens. The agent startup command (e.g. claude resume) runs in this directory. CwdChanged events from Zellij are not relevant for rally agents.

Plugin version gate: state_version must be Option<u64> (not u64) because daemon EventBus version starts at 0 for pre-existing SQLite data. A u64 default of 0 rejects the first snapshot. Fixed 2026-04-25.

S5 design decisions: (1) 'a' key opens action menu, 'K' (shift+k) for inbox ack. (2) Floating pane menu shells out to 'zellij action ...' CLI — no IPC back to plugin. Plugin passes pane_id + CWD as args when spawning the floating pane.
